explanation: The detection logic relies on an 'Image|endswith' check for '\net.exe' or '\net1.exe' combined with the command line argument 'time'. Copying the net.exe binary to an alternative directory and renaming it disrupts the 'selection_time' image match while leaving the functional capability intact.

The rule explicitly targets legacy native administrative binaries ('net.exe', 'net1.exe', 'w32tm.exe') commonly used for quick tactical time checks. Using native PowerShell cmdlets like 'Get-Date' or API calls inside a script discovers system time and time zone details seamlessly without triggering any of the defined utility signatures.
